rules.summary: Source paths expose named capabilities instead of generic technical layers.
Generic bucket filenames are forbidden.
Authored source directories use camelCase.
Capabilities call official services without pass-through wrapper layers.
Named functions use arrow constants declared before use.
Anonymous generators appear only as the direct Effect.gen callback.
Functions use cohesive inputs and no positional boolean flags.
A function performs one visible domain job without ceremonial wrappers.
Adjacent function declarations have one blank line between them.
Function control flow nests no more than two levels.
Schema.TaggedError is the only authored class form.
Every explicit loop has an immediately preceding intent comment.
Indexed non-null access has an immediately preceding proof comment.
Ordered multi-phase pipelines have a contract and numbered phases.
Do not export type-guard/parse helpers for schema-owned literals and numbers; decode with Schema or private switch inside hook readConfig only.
Runtime object types derive from executable Effect Schema definitions.
Interfaces are reserved for declaration-file augmentation.
Enums are forbidden.
Authored conditional and infer type machinery is forbidden.
Authored TypeScript assertions are forbidden.
TypeScript and tool suppression directives are forbidden.
Barrels contain direct wildcard exports only.
Identifiers use domain-specific names instead of vague generic roles.
Function inputs are never mutated.
Reduce is not used to build arrays or objects.
Application effects do not use Promise.all.
Effect.run calls exist only at src/cli/main.ts.
Capabilities use official Effect services without pass-through wrappers.
Installed hook graphs import only node modules and their runtime island.
Application modules do not import the installed hook runtime.
Application presentation routes through TerminalUI.
